1.Drew RomoLHBCWS vs Colin Rea (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.233 / xBA 0.272 / xSLG 0.455 (-82 pts)Park +7 pts14d 0.294vs RHP —K 19.8%BB 9.4%HR 5.9%Hit 12.2%Edge Pitcher

Drew Romo LHB vs Colin Rea RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 151+12+189, pitcher rows 729+2657+2656

Verdict

Rea's curveball is the projected matchup advantage, while Drew Romo should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.233 xwOBA (-82 pts vs league, Pitcher). Count-state blend: -118 pts (bb_type-adj 0.344 -> final 0.233); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Drew Romo is on a 14-day slump (-89 pts vs season). Colin Rea is in a 14-day rough patch (+96 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Career = batter's full platoon-filtered BBE distribution. Projected re-weights each pitch-specific BBE distribution by the pitcher's marginal pitch usage; pitches with <5 weighted BBE fall back to the batter's overall mix. Δ (pp) is the projected share minus career share, in percentage points. ★ rows mark the highlighted 10–26° barrel zone (overlaps Statcast's broader 8–32° sweet-spot range).

swing length 7.3 ft
attack angle 12.6°
PitchVAA (deg)Bat attack (deg)Swings (n)Match gap (deg)Note
4-Seam Fastball-4.6+5.816+1.2on plane
Split-Finger-7.7+12.6*5+4.9swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk

* fewer than 5 swings on this pitch type; showing batter's overall attack angle as a fallback.

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

2.Kevin McGonigleLHBDET vs Kevin Gausman (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.246 / xBA 0.237 / xSLG 0.371 (-69 pts)Park -4 pts14d 0.319vs RHP 0.292K 16.8%BB 9.2%HR 0.7%Hit 15.9%Edge Pitcher

Kevin McGonigle LHB vs Kevin Gausman RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 759+0+0, pitcher rows 820+3482+2959

Verdict

Projection: 0.246 xwOBA (-69 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -33 pts (raw 0.324 -> adj 0.291); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -40 pts (bb_type-adj 0.291 -> final 0.246); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Kevin McGonigle is on a 14-day slump (-58 pts vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

bat speed 70.2 mph
swing length 6.8 ft
attack angle 14.7°
PitchVAA (deg)Bat attack (deg)Swings (n)Match gap (deg)Note
4-Seam Fastball-5.0+9.640+4.6swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk

* fewer than 5 swings on this pitch type; showing batter's overall attack angle as a fallback.

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. James Outman LHB vs Robert Gasser LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 163+605+601, pitcher rows 0+166+399

Verdict

Gasser's sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while James Outman should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.249 xwOBA (-66 pts vs league, Pitcher). Count-state blend: -71 pts (bb_type-adj 0.311 -> final 0.249); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. LHB vs LHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Alec Bohm RHB vs Paul Skenes RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 552+1867+2170, pitcher rows 706+2997+2127

Verdict

Skenes' sweeper is the projected matchup advantage. Projection: 0.250 xwOBA (-65 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -37 pts (raw 0.282 -> adj 0.246); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Paul Skenes is in a 14-day hot stretch (-45 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

5.Owen CaissieLHBMIA vs Drew Rasmussen (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.253 / xBA 0.237 / xSLG 0.353 (-62 pts)Park -9 pts14d —vs RHP —K 28.9%BB 5.9%HR 2.3%Hit 19.6%Edge Pitcher

Owen Caissie LHB vs Drew Rasmussen RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 515+103+0, pitcher rows 603+2317+460

Verdict

Rasmussen's changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Owen Caissie should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.253 xwOBA (-62 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -12 pts (raw 0.302 -> adj 0.290); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -28 pts (bb_type-adj 0.290 -> final 0.253); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Drew Rasmussen is in a 14-day rough patch (+44 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

6.Christian VázquezRHBHOU vs Nathan Eovaldi (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.255 / xBA 0.206 / xSLG 0.321 (-60 pts)Park +2 pts14d 0.193vs RHP —K 22.7%BB 3.8%HR 2.4%Hit 19.5%Edge Pitcher

Christian Vázquez RHB vs Nathan Eovaldi RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 336+834+1141, pitcher rows 649+1924+2623

Verdict

Eovaldi's curveball is the projected matchup advantage, while Christian Vázquez should look to drive the slider. Projection: 0.255 xwOBA (-60 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -25 pts (raw 0.286 -> adj 0.261); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -8 pts (bb_type-adj 0.261 -> final 0.255); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Christian Vázquez is on a 14-day slump (-46 pts vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). PitchMarginal Usage %Batter xwOBAPitcher xwOBA allowedPitcher BB-mixAdj batter xwOBAAdj Δ (pts)Projected xwOBAProjected xBAProjected xSLGProjected Whiff %
Split-Finger29.40.3150.276GB 65% / LD 15% / FB 14% / PU 6%0.272-440.2320.1820.26521.2
4-Seam Fastball27.20.2920.296GB 58% / LD 21% / FB 17% / PU 4%0.279-130.2600.2150.3048.3

Slider3.70.3060.322GB 57% / LD 19% / FB 20% / PU 5%0.292-140.2990.2430.38718.7

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Coverage % = share of the pitcher's attack on this pitch landing in zones with batter data; remainder imputed at league baselines (xwOBA 0.315, xBA 0.245, xSLG 0.405) so two batters with different coverage maps stay comparable.

Bat-tracking interaction

bat speed 64.8 mph
swing length 6.6 ft
attack angle 7.5°
PitchVAA (deg)Bat attack (deg)Swings (n)Match gap (deg)Note
Split-Finger-7.6+14.88+7.2swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk
4-Seam Fastball-4.7+5.6106+0.8on plane
Curveball-9.5+14.09+4.4swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk
Cutter-5.7+8.828+3.1swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk
Sinker-5.2+3.154-2.1on plane
Slider-7.4+9.344+1.9on plane

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Coverage % = share of the pitcher's attack on this pitch landing in zones with batter data; remainder imputed at league baselines (xwOBA 0.315, xBA 0.245, xSLG 0.405) so two batters with different coverage maps stay comparable.

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

8.Chandler SimpsonLHBTB vs Eury Pérez (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.263 / xBA 0.208 / xSLG 0.388 (-52 pts)Park -25 pts14d 0.214vs RHP —K 17.5%BB 7.4%HR 0.0%Hit 22.7%Edge Pitcher

Chandler Simpson LHB vs Eury Pérez RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 566+1518+0, pitcher rows 803+1675+0

Verdict

Pérez's changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Chandler Simpson should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.263 xwOBA (-52 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -21 pts (raw 0.310 -> adj 0.289); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Chandler Simpson is on a 14-day slump (-41 pts vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

9.Maverick HandleyRHBBAL vs Miles Mikolas (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.267 / xBA 0.250 / xSLG 0.420 (-48 pts)Park -2 pts14d —vs RHP —K 17.2%BB 3.8%HR 0.0%Hit 27.3%Slight Pitcher

Maverick Handley RHB vs Miles Mikolas RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 0+183+0, pitcher rows 624+2487+2705

Verdict

Mikolas' 4-seam fastball is the projected matchup advantage, while Maverick Handley should look to drive the slider. Projection: 0.267 xwOBA (-48 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -5 pts (raw 0.317 -> adj 0.312); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -43 pts (bb_type-adj 0.312 -> final 0.267); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

10.J.T. RealmutoRHBPHI vs Paul Skenes (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.268 / xBA 0.204 / xSLG 0.309 (-47 pts)Park +2 pts14d 0.279vs RHP 0.220K 28.4%BB 2.8%HR 1.9%Hit 21.9%Slight Pitcher

J.T. Realmuto RHB vs Paul Skenes RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 367+2264+1662, pitcher rows 706+2997+2127

Verdict

Skenes' sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while J.T. Realmuto should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.268 xwOBA (-47 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -35 pts (raw 0.285 -> adj 0.250); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +16 pts (bb_type-adj 0.250 -> final 0.268); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Paul Skenes is in a 14-day hot stretch (-45 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

PitchMarginal Usage %Batter xwOBAPitcher xwOBA allowedPitcher BB-mixAdj batter xwOBAAdj Δ (pts)Projected xwOBAProjected xBAProjected xSLGProjected Whiff %
4-Seam Fastball32.20.3270.272GB 39% / LD 29% / FB 23% / PU 9%0.306-210.2630.2160.31512.4
Sweeper21.90.3080.257GB 33% / LD 15% / FB 35% / PU 16%0.249-590.1910.1430.26236.0
Sinker17.00.3400.303GB 70% / LD 21% / FB 6% / PU 3%0.287-530.2750.2510.3255.3

Slider6.80.3060.302GB 42% / LD 18% / FB 22% / PU 18%0.273-330.2610.2010.33427.2
Changeup3.70.3080.267GB 48% / LD 21% / FB 21% / PU 9%0.289-190.2410.1840.31563.8
Curveball2.30.3190.310GB 48% / LD 21% / FB 21% / PU 9%0.293-250.2880.2230.36230.0

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Career = batter's full platoon-filtered BBE distribution. Projected re-weights each pitch-specific BBE distribution by the pitcher's marginal pitch usage; pitches with <5 weighted BBE fall back to the batter's overall mix. Δ (pp) is the projected share minus career share, in percentage points. ★ rows mark the highlighted 10–26° barrel zone (overlaps Statcast's broader 8–32° sweet-spot range).

11.Liam HicksLHBMIA vs Drew Rasmussen (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.269 / xBA 0.228 / xSLG 0.364 (-46 pts)Park -10 pts14d 0.301vs RHP 0.321K 17.0%BB 7.0%HR 2.4%Hit 20.0%Slight Pitcher

Liam Hicks LHB vs Drew Rasmussen RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 552+1537+0, pitcher rows 603+2317+460

Verdict

Rasmussen's changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Liam Hicks should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.269 xwOBA (-46 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -24 pts (raw 0.312 -> adj 0.288); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -9 pts (bb_type-adj 0.288 -> final 0.269); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Liam Hicks is on a 14-day slump (-47 pts vs season). Drew Rasmussen is in a 14-day rough patch (+44 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

12.Christian WalkerRHBHOU vs Nathan Eovaldi (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.271 / xBA 0.211 / xSLG 0.308 (-44 pts)Park -6 pts14d 0.212vs RHP 0.217K 27.6%BB 4.1%HR 4.2%Hit 18.3%Slight Pitcher

Christian Walker RHB vs Nathan Eovaldi RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 678+2519+2326, pitcher rows 649+1924+2623

Verdict

Eovaldi's split-finger is the projected matchup advantage, while Christian Walker should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.271 xwOBA (-44 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -35 pts (raw 0.296 -> adj 0.261); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +16 pts (bb_type-adj 0.261 -> final 0.271); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Christian Walker is on a 14-day slump (-121 pts vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 0+426+348, pitcher rows 656+1970+1029

Verdict

Holmes' slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Nick Sogard should look to drive the cutter. Projection: 0.271 xwOBA (-44 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -7 pts (raw 0.307 -> adj 0.301); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -14 pts (bb_type-adj 0.301 -> final 0.271); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

bat speed 64.5 mph
swing length 6.9 ft
attack angle 15.5°
PitchVAA (deg)Bat attack (deg)Swings (n)Match gap (deg)Note
Slider-8.6+18.714+10.1swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk

* fewer than 5 swings on this pitch type; showing batter's overall attack angle as a fallback.

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

14.Leo JiménezRHBMIA vs Drew Rasmussen (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.273 / xBA 0.232 / xSLG 0.378 (-42 pts)Park -13 pts14d 0.392vs RHP —K 20.5%BB 3.8%HR 1.7%Hit 23.3%Slight Pitcher

Leo Jiménez RHB vs Drew Rasmussen RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 163+144+847, pitcher rows 603+2317+460

Verdict

Projection: 0.273 xwOBA (-42 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -14 pts (raw 0.308 -> adj 0.294); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -8 pts (bb_type-adj 0.294 -> final 0.273); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Drew Rasmussen is in a 14-day rough patch (+44 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

15.Alejandro OsunaLHBTEX vs Peter Lambert (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.273 / xBA 0.218 / xSLG 0.355 (-42 pts)Park -4 pts14d 0.330vs RHP 0.346K 22.1%BB 14.1%HR 0.3%Hit 16.8%Slight Pitcher

Alejandro Osuna LHB vs Peter Lambert RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 182+744+0, pitcher rows 478+0+1044

Verdict

Lambert's changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Alejandro Osuna should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.273 xwOBA (-42 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -20 pts (raw 0.315 -> adj 0.295); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -18 pts (bb_type-adj 0.295 -> final 0.273); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

16.Jhonny PeredaRHBSEA vs Lucas Giolito (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.274 / xBA 0.243 / xSLG 0.395 (-41 pts)Park -9 pts14d —vs RHP —K 18.7%BB 8.7%HR 0.0%Hit 21.4%Slight Pitcher

Jhonny Pereda RHB vs Lucas Giolito RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 31+333+158, pitcher rows 0+2372+0

Verdict

Giolito's slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Jhonny Pereda should look to drive the changeup. Projection: 0.274 xwOBA (-41 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -15 pts (raw 0.323 -> adj 0.308); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -26 pts (bb_type-adj 0.308 -> final 0.274); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

17.Xavier EdwardsLHBMIA vs Drew Rasmussen (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.275 / xBA 0.230 / xSLG 0.358 (-40 pts)Park -8 pts14d 0.323vs RHP 0.257K 16.7%BB 7.0%HR 0.9%Hit 21.8%Slight Pitcher

Xavier Edwards LHB vs Drew Rasmussen RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 723+2260+1192, pitcher rows 603+2317+460

Verdict

Rasmussen's cutter is the projected matchup advantage, while Xavier Edwards should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.275 xwOBA (-40 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -17 pts (raw 0.306 -> adj 0.289); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -6 pts (bb_type-adj 0.289 -> final 0.275); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Drew Rasmussen is in a 14-day rough patch (+44 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Projected slashxwOBAxBAxSLG

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

18.José AzócarRHBATL vs Brayan Bello (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.275 / xBA 0.246 / xSLG 0.334 (-40 pts)Park -11 pts14d —vs RHP —K 20.6%BB 5.6%HR 0.0%Hit 26.7%Slight Pitcher

José Azócar RHB vs Brayan Bello RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 42+67+287, pitcher rows 686+2726+2713

Verdict

Bello's sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while José Azócar should look to drive the cutter. Projection: 0.275 xwOBA (-40 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -25 pts (raw 0.311 -> adj 0.286); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Brayan Bello is in a 14-day hot stretch (-101 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

19.Andruw MonasterioRHBBOS vs Grant Holmes (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.275 / xBA 0.226 / xSLG 0.370 (-40 pts)Park -24 pts14d —vs RHP —K 27.3%BB 7.3%HR 1.9%Hit 19.5%Slight Pitcher

Andruw Monasterio RHB vs Grant Holmes RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 313+563+647, pitcher rows 656+1970+1029

Verdict

Holmes' slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Andruw Monasterio should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.275 xwOBA (-40 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -10 pts (raw 0.302 -> adj 0.292); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +7 pts (bb_type-adj 0.292 -> final 0.275); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. PitchMarginal Usage %Batter xwOBAPitcher xwOBA allowedPitcher BB-mixAdj batter xwOBAAdj Δ (pts)Projected xwOBAProjected xBAProjected xSLGProjected Whiff %
Slider44.80.3060.281GB 51% / LD 22% / FB 23% / PU 4%0.295-110.2610.2260.30542.7
4-Seam Fastball34.40.3110.334GB 32% / LD 20% / FB 37% / PU 11%0.290-210.3090.2050.42024.9
Cutter9.90.3020.345GB 32% / LD 32% / FB 32% / PU 4%0.334+320.3630.2850.48716.0

Changeup0.60.3090.311GB 45% / LD 22% / FB 26% / PU 7%0.301-90.2970.2250.37741.8

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Coverage % = share of the pitcher's attack on this pitch landing in zones with batter data; remainder imputed at league baselines (xwOBA 0.315, xBA 0.245, xSLG 0.405) so two batters with different coverage maps stay comparable.

Bat-tracking interaction

bat speed 66.0 mph
swing length 6.7 ft
attack angle 9.9°
PitchVAA (deg)Bat attack (deg)Swings (n)Match gap (deg)Note
Slider-8.6+16.628+7.9swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk
4-Seam Fastball-5.0+6.848+1.8on plane
Cutter-5.7+8.414+2.7on plane
Curveball-9.5+11.47+1.8on plane
Sinker-6.3+3.837-2.5on plane

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

20.Justin CrawfordLHBPHI vs Paul Skenes (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.276 / xBA 0.216 / xSLG 0.394 (-39 pts)Park +3 pts14d 0.280vs RHP 0.281K 28.7%BB 6.5%HR 2.1%Hit 20.6%Slight Pitcher

Justin Crawford LHB vs Paul Skenes RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 491+0+0, pitcher rows 706+2997+2127

Verdict

Skenes' changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Justin Crawford should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.276 xwOBA (-39 pts vs league, Pitcher). Count-state blend: -21 pts (bb_type-adj 0.294 -> final 0.276); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Paul Skenes is in a 14-day hot stretch (-45 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

21.Joey WiemerRHBWSH vs Brandon Young (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.276 / xBA 0.228 / xSLG 0.407 (-39 pts)Park -3 pts14d —vs RHP —K 26.7%BB 6.2%HR 2.5%Hit 23.1%Slight Pitcher

Joey Wiemer RHB vs Brandon Young RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 293+223+109, pitcher rows 431+941+0

Verdict

Young's 4-seam fastball is the projected matchup advantage, while Joey Wiemer should look to drive the cutter. Projection: 0.276 xwOBA (-39 pts vs league, Pitcher). Count-state blend: -24 pts (bb_type-adj 0.303 -> final 0.276); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Brandon Young is in a 14-day hot stretch (-65 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Career = batter's full platoon-filtered BBE distribution. Projected re-weights each pitch-specific BBE distribution by the pitcher's marginal pitch usage; pitches with <5 weighted BBE fall back to the batter's overall mix. Δ (pp) is the projected share minus career share, in percentage points. ★ rows mark the highlighted 10–26° barrel zone (overlaps Statcast's broader 8–32° sweet-spot range).

23.Isaac ParedesRHBHOU vs Nathan Eovaldi (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.278 / xBA 0.219 / xSLG 0.325 (-37 pts)Park +4 pts14d 0.278vs RHP 0.245K 22.3%BB 6.1%HR 3.4%Hit 18.8%Slight Pitcher

Isaac Paredes RHB vs Nathan Eovaldi RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 715+1944+2862, pitcher rows 649+1924+2623

Verdict

Eovaldi's split-finger is the projected matchup advantage, while Isaac Paredes should look to drive the cutter. Projection: 0.278 xwOBA (-37 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -15 pts (raw 0.293 -> adj 0.278); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

PitchMarginal Usage %Batter xwOBAPitcher xwOBA allowedPitcher BB-mixAdj batter xwOBAAdj Δ (pts)Projected xwOBAProjected xBAProjected xSLGProjected Whiff %
Split-Finger30.00.3010.276GB 65% / LD 15% / FB 14% / PU 6%0.280-210.2400.1900.26844.1
4-Seam Fastball27.30.3230.296GB 58% / LD 21% / FB 17% / PU 4%0.320-30.3010.2500.3405.1
Cutter15.90.3210.314GB 44% / LD 15% / FB 35% / PU 6%0.290-310.2890.2100.3847.2
Curveball14.60.3110.289GB 48% / LD 20% / FB 29% / PU 3%0.310-10.2840.2100.33539.7
Sinker8.70.3250.315GB 68% / LD 16% / FB 11% / PU 4%0.301-240.3010.2520.3402.4
Slider3.40.2960.322GB 57% / LD 19% / FB 20% / PU 5%0.276-200.2830.2320.35015.7

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

24.Zach ColeLHBHOU vs Nathan Eovaldi (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.280 / xBA 0.246 / xSLG 0.415 (-35 pts)Park -4 pts14d 0.339vs RHP 0.390K 26.6%BB 3.5%HR 5.5%Hit 19.0%Slight Pitcher

Zach Cole LHB vs Nathan Eovaldi RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 111+224+0, pitcher rows 649+1924+2623

Verdict

Eovaldi's split-finger is the projected matchup advantage, while Zach Cole should look to drive the cutter. Projection: 0.280 xwOBA (-35 pts vs league, Pitcher). Count-state blend: -27 pts (bb_type-adj 0.311 -> final 0.280); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

25.Darell HernaizRHBATH vs Adrian Houser (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.280 / xBA 0.244 / xSLG 0.349 (-35 pts)14d —vs RHP —K 13.2%BB 4.2%HR 1.2%Hit 26.3%Slight Pitcher

Darell Hernaiz RHB vs Adrian Houser RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 308+712+511, pitcher rows 661+1892+1131

Verdict

Houser's slider is the projected matchup advantage. Projection: 0.280 xwOBA (-35 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -11 pts (raw 0.298 -> adj 0.287); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -7 pts (bb_type-adj 0.287 -> final 0.280); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Adrian Houser is in a 14-day hot stretch (-40 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

26.Trea TurnerRHBPHI vs Paul Skenes (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.281 / xBA 0.221 / xSLG 0.340 (-34 pts)14d 0.267vs RHP 0.241K 25.9%BB 2.6%HR 2.4%Hit 24.1%Slight Pitcher

Trea Turner RHB vs Paul Skenes RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 702+2484+2122, pitcher rows 706+2997+2127

Verdict

Skenes' sweeper is the projected matchup advantage. Projection: 0.281 xwOBA (-34 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -13 pts (raw 0.281 -> adj 0.268); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +12 pts (bb_type-adj 0.268 -> final 0.281); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Paul Skenes is in a 14-day hot stretch (-45 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

27.Munetaka MurakamiLHBCWS vs Colin Rea (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.281 / xBA 0.296 / xSLG 0.455 (-34 pts)Park +6 pts14d 0.336vs RHP 0.351K 22.5%BB 11.5%HR 5.9%Hit 15.1%Slight Pitcher

Munetaka Murakami LHB vs Colin Rea RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 811+0+0, pitcher rows 729+2657+2656

Verdict

Rea's split-finger is the projected matchup advantage, while Munetaka Murakami should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.281 xwOBA (-34 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: +5 pts (raw 0.351 -> adj 0.357); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -81 pts (bb_type-adj 0.357 -> final 0.281); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Colin Rea is in a 14-day rough patch (+96 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

28.Vaughn GrissomRHBLAA vs Roki Sasaki (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.281 / xBA 0.245 / xSLG 0.424 (-34 pts)Park -7 pts14d 0.427vs RHP —K 15.3%BB 7.5%HR 4.6%Hit 17.0%Slight Pitcher

Vaughn Grissom RHB vs Roki Sasaki RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 327+0+444, pitcher rows 634+733+0

Verdict

Sasaki's slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Vaughn Grissom should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.281 xwOBA (-34 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -20 pts (raw 0.343 -> adj 0.323); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -35 pts (bb_type-adj 0.323 -> final 0.281); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Vaughn Grissom is on a 14-day heater (+60 pts vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

* fewer than 5 swings on this pitch type; showing batter's overall attack angle as a fallback.

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

29.Cam SmithRHBHOU vs Nathan Eovaldi (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.282 / xBA 0.213 / xSLG 0.341 (-33 pts)Park -7 pts14d 0.355vs RHP 0.342K 29.2%BB 4.8%HR 1.6%Hit 23.2%Slight Pitcher

Cam Smith RHB vs Nathan Eovaldi RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 698+2025+0, pitcher rows 649+1924+2623

Verdict

Eovaldi's split-finger is the projected matchup advantage, while Cam Smith should look to drive the cutter. Projection: 0.282 xwOBA (-33 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -22 pts (raw 0.297 -> adj 0.276); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +14 pts (bb_type-adj 0.276 -> final 0.282); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

30.Austin MartinRHBMIN vs Robert Gasser (LHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.284 / xBA 0.220 / xSLG 0.384 (-31 pts)Park +1 pts14d 0.312vs LHP —K 10.3%BB 4.6%HR 1.0%Hit 22.4%Slight Pitcher

Austin Martin RHB vs Robert Gasser LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 533+749+934, pitcher rows 0+166+399

Verdict

Gasser's sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while Austin Martin should look to drive the cutter. Projection: 0.284 xwOBA (-31 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -23 pts (raw 0.320 -> adj 0.297); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -14 pts (bb_type-adj 0.297 -> final 0.284); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Austin Martin is on a 14-day slump (-46 pts vs season). RHB vs LHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Bat-tracking interaction

bat speed 65.0 mph
swing length 6.7 ft
attack angle 7.7°
PitchVAA (deg)Bat attack (deg)Swings (n)Match gap (deg)Note
Sweeper-6.8+7.7*3+0.8on plane
4-Seam Fastball-4.3+2.542-1.9on plane
Sinker-5.0-0.718-5.7swing flatter than pitch — topped / grounder risk
Changeup-6.7+18.725+12.0swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk
Cutter-5.6+7.813+2.2on plane

* fewer than 5 swings on this pitch type; showing batter's overall attack angle as a fallback.

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

32.Adolis GarcíaRHBPHI vs Paul Skenes (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.285 / xBA 0.222 / xSLG 0.333 (-30 pts)Park +3 pts14d 0.250vs RHP 0.214K 30.1%BB 2.6%HR 3.5%Hit 22.7%Slight Pitcher

Adolis García RHB vs Paul Skenes RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 708+2154+2542, pitcher rows 706+2997+2127

Verdict

Skenes' sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while Adolis García should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.285 xwOBA (-30 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -17 pts (raw 0.284 -> adj 0.267); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +16 pts (bb_type-adj 0.267 -> final 0.285); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Adolis García is on a 14-day slump (-56 pts vs season). Paul Skenes is in a 14-day hot stretch (-45 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

bat speed 70.2 mph
swing length 7.6 ft
attack angle 9.2°
PitchVAA (deg)Bat attack (deg)Swings (n)Match gap (deg)Note
4-Seam Fastball-4.7+6.2262+1.5on plane
Sweeper-6.9+13.972+7.0swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk

Curveball-8.3+14.931+6.6swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

33.Brandon MarshLHBPHI vs Paul Skenes (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.285 / xBA 0.213 / xSLG 0.354 (-30 pts)Park -2 pts14d 0.361vs RHP 0.395K 30.0%BB 5.9%HR 2.1%Hit 22.9%Slight Pitcher

Brandon Marsh LHB vs Paul Skenes RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 538+1724+2062, pitcher rows 706+2997+2127

Verdict

Skenes' split-finger is the projected matchup advantage, while Brandon Marsh should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.285 xwOBA (-30 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -22 pts (raw 0.296 -> adj 0.274); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +13 pts (bb_type-adj 0.274 -> final 0.285); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Paul Skenes is in a 14-day hot stretch (-45 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 165+1459+1765, pitcher rows 363+2394+3247

Wheeler's sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while Jared Triolo should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.286 xwOBA (-29 pts vs league, Pitcher). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

PitchMarginal Usage %Batter xwOBAPitcher xwOBA allowedPitcher BB-mixAdj batter xwOBAAdj Δ (pts)Projected xwOBAProjected xBAProjected xSLGProjected Whiff %

Cutter0.10.3100.248GB 49% / LD 22% / FB 22% / PU 7%0.295-150.2280.1850.29117.1

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Career = batter's full platoon-filtered BBE distribution. Projected re-weights each pitch-specific BBE distribution by the pitcher's marginal pitch usage; pitches with <5 weighted BBE fall back to the batter's overall mix. Δ (pp) is the projected share minus career share, in percentage points. ★ rows mark the highlighted 10–26° barrel zone (overlaps Statcast's broader 8–32° sweet-spot range).

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

37.Jacob YoungRHBWSH vs Brandon Young (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.290 / xBA 0.223 / xSLG 0.380 (-25 pts)Park -3 pts14d 0.239vs RHP 0.191K 19.3%BB 4.7%HR 0.3%Hit 27.4%Even

Jacob Young RHB vs Brandon Young RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 569+1368+1956, pitcher rows 431+941+0

Verdict

Young's 4-seam fastball is the projected matchup advantage, while Jacob Young should look to drive the cutter. Projection: 0.290 xwOBA (-25 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -11 pts (raw 0.299 -> adj 0.288); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +5 pts (bb_type-adj 0.288 -> final 0.290); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Jacob Young is on a 14-day slump (-44 pts vs season). Brandon Young is in a 14-day hot stretch (-65 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

38.Jakob MarseeLHBMIA vs Drew Rasmussen (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.291 / xBA 0.254 / xSLG 0.394 (-24 pts)Park -15 pts14d 0.269vs RHP 0.250K 23.9%BB 8.3%HR 0.9%Hit 21.5%Even

Jakob Marsee LHB vs Drew Rasmussen RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 777+881+0, pitcher rows 603+2317+460

Verdict

Rasmussen's cutter is the projected matchup advantage, while Jakob Marsee should look to drive the curveball. Projection: 0.291 xwOBA (-24 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: +30 pts (raw 0.290 -> adj 0.320); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -14 pts (bb_type-adj 0.320 -> final 0.291); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Drew Rasmussen is in a 14-day rough patch (+44 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). PitchMarginal Usage %Batter xwOBAPitcher xwOBA allowedPitcher BB-mixAdj batter xwOBAAdj Δ (pts)Projected xwOBAProjected xBAProjected xSLGProjected Whiff %
Slider34.90.2940.295GB 53% / LD 21% / FB 22% / PU 4%0.272-220.2510.2110.30924.6
Sinker30.60.3290.306GB 52% / LD 18% / FB 24% / PU 6%0.318-110.3090.2320.3642.5

Sweeper0.10.3090.308GB 55% / LD 19% / FB 22% / PU 4%0.289-200.2820.2350.34314.3
Pitch Out0.10.3200.308GB 55% / LD 19% / FB 22% / PU 4%0.297-230.2890.2450.34510.6

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

40.Santiago EspinalRHBLAD vs Grayson Rodriguez (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.291 / xBA 0.259 / xSLG 0.407 (-24 pts)Park -23 pts14d —vs RHP —K 18.7%BB 4.7%HR 0.0%Hit 26.2%Even

Santiago Espinal RHB vs Grayson Rodriguez RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 153+1303+1512, pitcher rows 0+0+1879

Verdict

Rodriguez's slider is the projected matchup advantage. Projection: 0.291 xwOBA (-24 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: +9 pts (raw 0.306 -> adj 0.315); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. 41.Jorge MateoRHBATL vs Brayan Bello (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.292 / xBA 0.266 / xSLG 0.418 (-23 pts)Park -23 pts14d —vs RHP —K 22.5%BB 5.0%HR 0.4%Hit 25.3%Even

Jorge Mateo RHB vs Brayan Bello RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 232+336+820, pitcher rows 686+2726+2713

Verdict

Bello's sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while Jorge Mateo should look to drive the cutter. Projection: 0.292 xwOBA (-23 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: +21 pts (raw 0.305 -> adj 0.326); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -11 pts (bb_type-adj 0.326 -> final 0.292); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Brayan Bello is in a 14-day hot stretch (-101 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

PitchVAA (deg)Bat attack (deg)Swings (n)Match gap (deg)Note

* fewer than 5 swings on this pitch type; showing batter's overall attack angle as a fallback.

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. 42.Connor NorbyRHBMIA vs Drew Rasmussen (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.293 / xBA 0.237 / xSLG 0.359 (-22 pts)Park -11 pts14d 0.217vs RHP —K 24.8%BB 3.1%HR 2.4%Hit 24.6%Even

Connor Norby RHB vs Drew Rasmussen RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 572+1245+759, pitcher rows 603+2317+460

Verdict

Rasmussen's 4-seam fastball is the projected matchup advantage, while Connor Norby should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.293 xwOBA (-22 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -31 pts (raw 0.314 -> adj 0.282); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +21 pts (bb_type-adj 0.282 -> final 0.293); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Connor Norby is on a 14-day slump (-73 pts vs season). Drew Rasmussen is in a 14-day rough patch (+44 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

43.Keibert RuizRHBWSH vs Brandon Young (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.293 / xBA 0.230 / xSLG 0.384 (-22 pts)Park -2 pts14d —vs RHP —K 15.1%BB 3.4%HR 2.5%Hit 24.8%Even

Keibert Ruiz RHB vs Brandon Young RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 279+889+1662, pitcher rows 431+941+0

Verdict

Young's slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Keibert Ruiz should look to drive the cutter. Projection: 0.293 xwOBA (-22 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -13 pts (raw 0.304 -> adj 0.291); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Brandon Young is in a 14-day hot stretch (-65 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

44.Sebastián RiveroRHBLAA vs Roki Sasaki (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.294 / xBA 0.244 / xSLG 0.383 (-21 pts)Park -3 pts14d —vs RHP —K 18.8%BB 6.4%HR 0.0%Hit 26.5%Even

Sebastián Rivero RHB vs Roki Sasaki RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 120+127+0, pitcher rows 634+733+0

Verdict

Sasaki's slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Sebastián Rivero should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.294 xwOBA (-21 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -12 pts (raw 0.317 -> adj 0.305); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -8 pts (bb_type-adj 0.305 -> final 0.294); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

45.Sal FrelickLHBMIL vs Bailey Ober (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.294 / xBA 0.216 / xSLG 0.389 (-21 pts)Park +4 pts14d 0.250vs RHP 0.271K 12.4%BB 4.9%HR 2.5%Hit 24.1%Even

Sal Frelick LHB vs Bailey Ober RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 587+2436+2087, pitcher rows 776+2356+2697

Verdict

Ober's changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Sal Frelick should look to drive the curveball. Projection: 0.294 xwOBA (-21 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -8 pts (raw 0.294 -> adj 0.286); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Bailey Ober is in a 14-day hot stretch (-77 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

PitchMarginal Usage %Batter xwOBAPitcher xwOBA allowedPitcher BB-mixAdj batter xwOBAAdj Δ (pts)Projected xwOBAProjected xBAProjected xSLGProjected Whiff %
4-Seam Fastball40.50.3100.319GB 24% / LD 21% / FB 38% / PU 17%0.300-100.3040.2300.3964.3

Sinker0.10.3150.294GB 30% / LD 21% / FB 35% / PU 14%0.309-60.2880.2110.3892.7

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

PitchVAA (deg)Bat attack (deg)Swings (n)Match gap (deg)Note
4-Seam Fastball-4.8+3.4261-1.4on plane

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

46.Connor JoeRHBSEA vs Lucas Giolito (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.296 / xBA 0.260 / xSLG 0.405 (-19 pts)Park -16 pts14d —vs RHP —K 17.6%BB 9.1%HR 1.0%Hit 21.8%Even

Connor Joe RHB vs Lucas Giolito RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 130+334+1655, pitcher rows 0+2372+0

Verdict

Giolito's slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Connor Joe should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.296 xwOBA (-19 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -6 pts (raw 0.326 -> adj 0.320); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -9 pts (bb_type-adj 0.320 -> final 0.296); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

47.Anthony VolpeRHBNYY vs Freddy Peralta (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.296 / xBA 0.207 / xSLG 0.357 (-19 pts)Park -2 pts14d —vs RHP —K 30.3%BB 6.5%HR 2.9%Hit 21.1%Even

Anthony Volpe RHB vs Freddy Peralta RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 18+2511+3076, pitcher rows 762+3365+3151

Verdict

Peralta's slider is the projected matchup advantage. Projection: 0.296 xwOBA (-19 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -8 pts (raw 0.292 -> adj 0.284); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +14 pts (bb_type-adj 0.284 -> final 0.296); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. PitchMarginal Usage %Batter xwOBAPitcher xwOBA allowedPitcher BB-mixAdj batter xwOBAAdj Δ (pts)Projected xwOBAProjected xBAProjected xSLGProjected Whiff %

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Bat-tracking interaction

bat speed 69.1 mph
swing length 7.1 ft
attack angle 9.7°
PitchVAA (deg)Bat attack (deg)Swings (n)Match gap (deg)Note
4-Seam Fastball-4.3+7.1202+2.8on plane
Slider-7.5+12.5124+5.0swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk
Changeup-6.8+15.737+8.9swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk
Curveball-8.9+16.129+7.2swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk
Slow Curve-9.9+9.7*0-0.2on plane
Sweeper-8.2+14.164+6.0swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk

* fewer than 5 swings on this pitch type; showing batter's overall attack angle as a fallback.

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

48.Elias DíazRHBKC vs Andre Pallante (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.296 / xBA 0.230 / xSLG 0.359 (-19 pts)Park -3 pts14d —vs RHP —K 17.7%BB 7.4%HR 3.3%Hit 20.4%Even

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 74+1056+1337, pitcher rows 728+2722+1981

Verdict

Pallante's knuckle curve is the projected matchup advantage, while Elias Díaz should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.296 xwOBA (-19 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -14 pts (raw 0.305 -> adj 0.291); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +8 pts (bb_type-adj 0.291 -> final 0.296); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Coverage % = share of the pitcher's attack on this pitch landing in zones with batter data; remainder imputed at league baselines (xwOBA 0.315, xBA 0.245, xSLG 0.405) so two batters with different coverage maps stay comparable.

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

49.Carlos NarváezRHBBOS vs Grant Holmes (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.298 / xBA 0.228 / xSLG 0.389 (-17 pts)Park -17 pts14d —vs RHP —K 29.0%BB 8.6%HR 2.6%Hit 20.5%Even

Carlos Narváez RHB vs Grant Holmes RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 358+1884+68, pitcher rows 656+1970+1029

Verdict

Holmes' slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Carlos Narváez should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.298 xwOBA (-17 pts vs league, Pitcher). Count-state blend: +11 pts (bb_type-adj 0.303 -> final 0.298); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

50.Cedric MullinsLHBTB vs Eury Pérez (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.298 / xBA 0.220 / xSLG 0.392 (-17 pts)Park -11 pts14d 0.179vs RHP —K 27.4%BB 10.7%HR 4.7%Hit 17.3%Even

Verdict

Pérez's changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Cedric Mullins should look to drive the curveball. Projection: 0.298 xwOBA (-17 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: +10 pts (raw 0.295 -> adj 0.305); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Coverage % = share of the pitcher's attack on this pitch landing in zones with batter data; remainder imputed at league baselines (xwOBA 0.315, xBA 0.245, xSLG 0.405) so two batters with different coverage maps stay comparable.

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).
